summary: Stancil uses microwaves as a metaphor for data: ubiquitous, useful, but potentially capped in transformative value. He argues data tooling may have hit a plateau as a utilitarian appliance, and that only a shift to fast, natural‑language access (via LLMs) could unlock a true second act by empowering many more people to ask and answer questions.
money_quotes: What’s notable about this story is that it’s not declaring the microwave dead; to the contrary, the microwave is everywhere. It’s obituary for the microwave’s potential.
There’s a different future that’s also out there: One in which data is everywhere, but the revolution is nowhere.
In our 72 attempts to redefine BI, we still haven’t broken the dashboarding barrier.
The only interface that will let people get the answers fast enough and painlessly enough to break this cycle is natural language.
If there’s value in our data, it’s the people that will find it.
key_insight: Data may be stuck as a commodity appliance unless LLM‑driven, natural‑language BI enables mass participation in analysis.
